regime_justification: USDJPY pinned at 160, BOJ rate hike priced 97% for June 15-16, May intervention still fresh, vol subdued despite proximity to known catalyst—classic pre-intervention standoff.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY testing 160 watch-line, BOJ meeting 6 days away with 97% hike priced, JGB 10Y at 2.67%, vol suppressed—markets coiled ahead of twin catalysts.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting June 15-16: 97% hike odds, USDJPY at 160 trigger line, suppressed vol masks elevated risk.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ volatility tail; if 25bp hike delivered, carry unwind history suggests 23-31% BTC corrections follow.

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: If BOJ normalizes smoothly and no follow-on MOF action, risk dissipates; scenario-weighted lower.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: One-month forward: assuming BOJ hike absorbed, spread still ~190bp supports carry unless oil/geopolitics reignite.

bottom_line: Stage 2: Pre-Intervention standoff. USDJPY pinned at 160 with BOJ June 15-16 meeting 97% priced for 25bp hike to 1.0%. History warns: every BOJ hike since Mar-24 triggered 23-31% BTC corrections. Vol is eerily calm (USDJPY 1M IV data unavailable but VIX 18.6, MOVE 75). Grades C/C/D/D reflect binary catalyst risk inside 1-2 weeks, then scenario-weighted fade if hike lands smoothly. Watch: BOJ tone on further hikes, MOF rhetoric post-decision, and whether vol stays suppressed through the event. May intervention (≈$35bn) bought time; June meeting is the pressure valve. Oil easing (WTI ~91) and Iran ceasefire chatter help contain ¥/bbl stress. BTC ~63.7k, down 17% weekly, correlating tightly with risk-off. Maintain defensive posture through June 16; reassess post-event.

stack.note: Pinned at MOF intervention watch-line; 18 pips from 160.00 threshold
Holding near 2-week high post jobs data; Fed hike odds rising
Eased from 2.7% on Iran ceasefire hopes; still elevated near multi-year highs
Spread widening on US yield rise; still wide enough to support carry structurally
Rates vol picking up but still well below stress threshold (100+)
Collapsed from 21.5; complacency into BOJ/Fed twin events
Off highs on Iran-Israel ceasefire signals; ¥/bbl pressure easing
Down 17% weekly; Strategy resumed buying, but institutional flows weak
Rebound from 3-day selloff; chipmakers recovering after -8% Seoul rout

trigger_map.note: 97% priced; history: Mar-24/Jul-24/Jan-25/Dec-25 hikes → 23-31% BTC corrections. Event 6 days out.
USDJPY at 160 watch-line; May intervention ≈$35bn still in memory. Mimura rhetoric remains hawkish.
Currently 2.67%; hit 2.8% on May 18 (29-yr high). BOJ may adjust bond taper if yields spike post-hike.
Currently 75; watching but no imminent stress. Rates vol contained ahead of BOJ/Fed.
Iran-Israel ceasefire chatter easing oil; Hormuz traffic still 90-95% below normal but stabilizing.

narrative.p1: USDJPY pinned at 160.15, testing the MOF watch-line as markets enter the 6-day countdown to the BOJ's June 15-16 meeting. Bloomberg reports 97% odds of a 25bp hike to 1.0%, the highest policy rate in nearly 30 years. JGB 10Y eased 5bp to 2.67% on Iran-Israel ceasefire signals (oil off highs), but remains elevated. US 10Y jumped to 4.55% on strong payrolls, widening the spread to 188bp. VIX collapsed 13.7% to 18.6—striking complacency given the twin BOJ/Fed catalysts inside two weeks. Nikkei rebounded +2.1% after a brutal 3-day tech selloff mirroring Friday's Wall Street chipmaker rout. No stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage-2; the setup remains a pre-intervention standoff.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 188bp, carry-to-vol unavailable (USDJPY 1M IV data not retrieved), ¥/bbl ≈14,602 (WTI $91.43 × 160.15), USDJPY 5d +0.58%, stretched vs 60d mean but not extreme. We remain Stage 2: Pre-Intervention. BOJ meeting is the binary. History is unambiguous: every BOJ hike since March 2024 triggered 23-31% BTC corrections (Crypto Rover analysis, BeInCrypto). Current setup: hike 97% priced, yet vol is eerily low (VIX 18.6, MOVE 75). That divergence flags either complacency or confidence the hike is fully discounted. 1-week grade C (Watchful): BOJ delivery risk + any hawkish guidance on further hikes could reignite unwind. 2-week grade C: post-BOJ vol tail persists. Applied unwind prior to 2w: ambiguous data (vol low, but event binary) leans one notch more cautious than a pure D. If BOJ delivers 25bp with dovish tone and no MOF follow-on, risk dissipates quickly—hence 3w/4w both D. Spread at 188bp still structurally wide enough to support carry unless oil/geopolitics reignite or Fed surprises hawkish.
narrative.p3: BTC $63.7k, up 2.9% today but down 17% over 7 days—classic risk-off deleveraging. Strategy Inc resumed $100M BTC buying (CoinDesk), but open interest data unavailable and institutional flows remain weak (ETF outflows cited by Bybit). BTC-DXY correlation data not retrieved, but qualitatively BTC is tracking equities and inverse yen. The BOJ effect looms large: if June 16 delivers a hawkish surprise (e.g., signal of further hikes into H2), carry unwind could cascade into crypto liquidity withdrawal. For now, BTC is consolidating near the 200-week MA (~$63k per CoinDesk)—a historical cycle pivot. XRP data not retrieved. Crypto positioning: remain defensive through June 16; any rally into the event is a fade candidate. Post-BOJ, if hike is dovish and USDJPY stabilizes below 158, risk-on rotation could support a relief bounce. Until then, treat strength as distribution.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 ends June 16 (BOJ decision day). 97% hike priced, but tone on future hikes and bond taper review (scheduled for June MPM per BOJ) are wild cards. Vol is too low for a binary event—either markets are right and it's priced, or a volatility explosion awaits. Week 2 carries the post-event tail: if BOJ signals more hikes ahead or MOF rhetoric stays hawkish, unwind risk persists. History shows BTC corrections materialize in the 1-2 weeks post-BOJ-hike, not always same-day.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: If BOJ hike lands dovish (one-and-done tone) and USDJPY stabilizes 155-158, Week 3 sees risk fade as carry traders re-assess. US CPI/PPI data due this week (June 11-12 per CNBC) will also recalibrate Fed hike odds, which are currently 70% for December. Lower inflation print would support longer-duration assets and ease pressure on yen. Scenario-weighted lower risk: base case is BOJ normalizes without shock.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: By Week 4 (early July), assuming BOJ delivered 25bp and no follow-on intervention, carry regime reasserts if spread holds >180bp and oil stays contained. Geopolitical tail (Hormuz still 90-95% below normal traffic per OilPrice.com) remains a latent trigger, but ceasefire chatter is constructive. Fed June meeting (likely hold) will be in the rear-view. Unless energy or China macro surprises negatively, unwind risk is low by end-month.

watch_list: BOJ decision June 15-16: hike size (25bp expected), tone on further hikes, bond taper interim assessment
USDJPY post-BOJ: does it hold below 160 or break higher? MOF intervention threshold unclear but May action at 160+ suggests low tolerance.
US CPI (June 11) / PPI (June 12): if softer than expected, Fed hike odds fall, DXY weakens, yen benefits—unwind risk drops.
JGB 10Y: break above 2.8% would signal fiscal/inflation stress, potentially forcing BOJ into more aggressive taper or yield curve control adjustment.
Iran-Israel: ceasefire holding or renewed strikes? Oil at $91 is manageable; spike to $100+ reignites ¥/bbl pressure and carry unwind thesis.
